page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine configured with 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Built on the Intel x86_64 architecture, the instance utilizes 16 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled to deliver dedicated compute resources. The processor configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the n4-highcpu-32 demonstrates strong performance characteristics. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1900.0 and a multi-core score of 16160.0, both placing in the top tier of its class. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 227.19 GB/sec for large-block reads and 104.22 GB/sec for large-block writes. The system also excels in static web serving throughput at 49.77 GB/sec and shows strong capabilities in image processing and text processing. Supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, the processor handles vector and matrix operations efficiently, enabling a text generation speed of 270.45 tokens/sec for small language models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-highcpu-32 offers a balanced profile for workloads that demand high compute density without the overhead of excess memory. By avoiding the cost of unneeded RAM or bundled storage, it provides a streamlined resource allocation. This makes the instance particularly well-suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, image processing, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM on the Intel x86_64 architecture. Utilizing 16 physical cores with Hyper-Threading, this instance provides dedicated CPU resources supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers strong performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core benchmarks, large-block memory bandwidth, and static web serving. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 instruction sets enhances its capability for vector and matrix calculations. This resource profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-intensive tasks that do not require large memory capacities, making it an excellent fit for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel x86_64 processor. It delivers strong performance in web serving and image processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-32 is an Intel-based compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong web serving performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors, advanced matrix extensions, and high-throughput performance for compute-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM powered by Intel x86_64 architecture
Dedicated CPU allocation with 16 physical cores and 260 MB L3 cache
Strong performance in Geekbench single-core, multi-core, and image processing
Equipped with Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated computing
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and lightweight ML inference
